landing.hero.subtitle: Connect a repository and let LuxView detect the stack, publish the route, provision HTTPS, and keep runtime, logs, and services in one surface
landing.hero.surfaceTitle: Build, routing, and runtime in one panel
landing.hero.surfaceSubtitle: Watch the deploy happen, confirm HTTPS, track app health, and keep rollback within reach.
landing.stacks.title: Works with your stack
landing.stacks.subtitle: Stack detection, build pipeline, and runtime orchestration shaped around the ecosystems teams actually ship.
landing.features.title: Deploy, runtime, and operations in one place
landing.features.subtitle: LuxView Cloud replaces scattered tooling with one surface to publish, protect, and operate applications on your own infrastructure.
landing.features.autoDetect.title: Auto-detect Stack
landing.features.autoDetect.description: Node.js, Python, Go, Rust, Java, Docker, or static — detected automatically from your repo.
landing.features.ssl.title: Free SSL Certificates
landing.features.ssl.description: Every app gets HTTPS via Let's Encrypt. Auto-renewed, zero configuration needed.
landing.features.subdomains.title: Instant Subdomains
landing.features.subdomains.description: yourapp.luxview.cloud is live the moment your build finishes. Custom domains coming soon.
landing.features.databases.title: Managed Databases
landing.features.databases.description: PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB, RabbitMQ — provisioned in one click, credentials auto-injected.
landing.features.monitoring.title: Real-time Monitoring
landing.features.monitoring.description: CPU, memory, runtime logs, and custom alerts — all live in the dashboard.
landing.features.autoDeploy.title: Auto-deploy & Rollback
landing.features.autoDeploy.description: Push to main triggers a deploy. Roll back to any previous version in one click.
landing.howItWorks.title: From repository to live route
landing.howItWorks.subtitle: A tighter flow to ship with less friction and more operational visibility.
landing.howItWorks.connectGithub.title: Connect GitHub
landing.howItWorks.connectGithub.descrip: Sign in with your GitHub account and authorize LuxView to access your repositories.
landing.howItWorks.pickConfigure.title: Pick & Configure
landing.howItWorks.pickConfigure.descrip: Select a repo, choose a branch, set environment variables, and pick a subdomain.
landing.howItWorks.shipIt.title: Ship It
landing.howItWorks.shipIt.description: Hit deploy. We build, provision SSL, start your container, and route traffic — all automatically.
landing.pricing.title: Plans tied to real infrastructure limits
landing.pricing.subtitle: Each plan turns CPU, memory, services, and automation into a clear envelope for your VPS.
landing.pricing.footer: Deploy on your own VPS. Full control, no vendor lock-in.
landing.footer.description: Self-hosted PaaS built for operational clarity. Publish, monitor, and adjust your applications from a single control tower.
landing.footer.builtWith: Built with Go + React + Docker + Traefik
status.title: System Status
docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Everything you need to deploy and manage applications on LuxView Cloud.
docs.gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
docs.gettingStarted.description: First steps to deploy your first app
docs.gettingStarted.step1: Sign in with GitHub — no passwords, just OAuth
docs.gettingStarted.step2: Select a repository and branch from your GitHub account
docs.gettingStarted.step3: Configure subdomain, environment variables, and resource limits
docs.gettingStarted.step4: Deploy — stack is auto-detected, image is built, HTTPS is provisioned
docs.deploying.title: Deploying Apps
docs.deploying.envVars: Environment variables are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) and injected at deploy time
docs.deploying.autoDeploy: Auto-deploy: push to your branch and GitHub webhook triggers a new deploy
docs.deploying.rollback: One-click rollback to any previous successful deployment
docs.deploying.monitoring: Real-time CPU, memory, and network metrics with configurable alerts
docs.services.title: Managed Services
docs.services.description: Databases, caches, message brokers, and storage
docs.services.postgres: PostgreSQL — relational database with built-in SQL editor and table browser
docs.services.redis: Redis — in-memory cache with isolated DB number per app
docs.services.mongo: MongoDB — document database with dedicated user per app
docs.services.rabbitmq: RabbitMQ — message broker with isolated vhost per app
docs.services.s3: S3 Storage (MinIO) — object storage with file browser, upload, and download
docs.stacks.title: Supported Stacks
docs.stacks.nodejs: Node.js — detected via package.json (Express, Next.js, Fastify, etc.)
docs.stacks.python: Python — detected via requirements.txt or Pipfile (Django, Flask, FastAPI)
docs.stacks.java: Java — detected via pom.xml or build.gradle (Spring Boot, Quarkus)
docs.stacks.docker: Docker — uses your Dockerfile directly (takes priority over other detections)
docs.stacks.static: Static — detected via index.html (served by Nginx)
docs.security.title: Security
docs.security.encryption: Credentials encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM, decrypted only at deploy time
docs.security.ssl: Automatic SSL via Let's Encrypt for every subdomain
docs.security.isolation: Each service has strict per-app isolation (dedicated DB, user, vhost, or bucket)
docs.security.oauth: GitHub OAuth — no passwords stored, JWT with HTTP-only secure cookies
docs.selfHosting.title: Self-Hosting
docs.selfHosting.description: Deploy LuxView on your own VPS
docs.selfHosting.requirements: Minimum: Ubuntu 22.04, 2 CPU, 4 GB RAM, Docker, wildcard DNS
docs.selfHosting.setup: Run setup-vps.sh to install Docker, configure firewall, and prepare the server
docs.selfHosting.dns: Point your domain + *.domain to your VPS IP address
docs.selfHosting.deploy: Configure .env, run make prod, then make migrate — platform is live
docs.api.title: API Reference
docs.api.description: REST API endpoints for apps, services, and metrics
docs.api.auth: Authentication via GitHub OAuth, JWT cookies, and /api/auth/me for user info
docs.api.apps: CRUD for apps, deploy, restart, stop, logs streaming (SSE), subdomain check
docs.api.services: Provision PostgreSQL, Redis, MongoDB, RabbitMQ, S3 — auto-injects env vars
docs.api.metrics: Real-time CPU/memory metrics, alert configuration, deployment history

deploy.wizard.prSummary.title: Pull Request Created
deploy.wizard.prSummary.subtitle: Code changes were generated and a PR was created in your repository
deploy.wizard.prSummary.warningDetail: The generated code changes add service connection configuration to your app. Deploying without merging the PR may cause runtime errors.
deploy.wizard.prSummary.step1: Open the PR link above and review the changes
deploy.wizard.prSummary.step2: Merge the PR into your branch
deploy.wizard.prSummary.step3: Click Continue to proceed to deploy

settings.replayToursDescription: Reset all guided tours so you can see them again
tour.dashboard.welcome.title: Welcome to your Dashboard!
tour.dashboard.welcome.content: This is your command center. Here you can see all your apps, recent deploys, and quick stats at a glance.
tour.dashboard.stats.title: Quick Stats
tour.dashboard.stats.content: These cards show your total apps, running instances, provisioned resources, and recent deploy activity.
tour.dashboard.newApp.title: Deploy a New App
tour.dashboard.newApp.content: Click here to deploy a new application from your GitHub repositories.
tour.dashboard.apps.title: Your Applications
tour.dashboard.apps.content: All your deployed applications appear here with real-time status, metrics, and quick actions.
tour.dashboard.deploys.title: Recent Deploys
tour.dashboard.deploys.content: Track your latest deployments. Click any deploy to jump to that app's details.
tour.newApp.welcome.title: Deploy a New App
tour.newApp.welcome.content: Follow the wizard to deploy an application from your GitHub repository in just a few steps.
tour.newApp.repos.title: Select a Repository
tour.newApp.repos.content: Choose from your GitHub repositories. We'll auto-detect the stack and configure the build.
tour.newApp.config.title: Configure Deployment
tour.newApp.config.content: Set the branch, subdomain, and environment variables for your deployment.
tour.appDetail.welcome.title: App Details
tour.appDetail.welcome.content: This is the control panel for your application. Manage deployments, logs, services, and settings.
tour.appDetail.overview.title: Overview
tour.appDetail.overview.content: See your app's status, stack, branch, resource usage, and uptime at a glance.
tour.appDetail.metrics.title: Live Metrics
tour.appDetail.metrics.content: Monitor CPU, memory, and network usage in real-time with interactive charts.
tour.appDetail.logs.title: Logs
tour.appDetail.logs.content: View runtime logs and build output. Filter and search through your application's output.
tour.appDetail.services.title: Managed Services
tour.appDetail.services.content: Add databases, caches, and message brokers. Credentials are auto-injected into your app.
tour.appDetail.deploys.title: Deployments
tour.appDetail.deploys.content: View deployment history and roll back to any previous version with one click.
tour.resources.welcome.title: Resources Overview
tour.resources.welcome.content: Here you can see all provisioned services across your apps — databases, caches, queues, and storage.
tour.resources.services.title: Your Services
tour.resources.services.content: Each card shows connection details and credentials. Click to explore databases or browse storage files.
tour.resources.addService.title: Adding Services
tour.resources.addService.content: To add a new service, go to an app's Services tab and click 'Add Service'.
tour.settings.theme.title: Theme
tour.settings.theme.content: Switch between dark and light mode to match your preference.
tour.settings.language.title: Language
tour.settings.language.content: Choose your preferred language. The entire dashboard will be translated.
tour.settings.tours.title: Replay Tutorials
tour.settings.tours.content: Reset all guided tours if you want to see them again.
analyze.title: Repository Analysis
analyze.deployFlow.title: Preparing your deployment
analyze.deployFlow.patience: This will only take a moment...
analyze.deployFlow.patienceAuto: This may take a few minutes — we're migrating your services...
analyze.serviceRecommendationsDescriptio: We detected external services that can be replaced with managed alternatives on LuxView Cloud.
analyze.migrationMode.autoDescription: Provisions the service, adapts your code, and creates a PR
analyze.migrationMode.manualDescription: Step-by-step instructions for you to do it yourself
analyze.prCreated: Migration PR created and opened in a new tab
analyze.loading.patience: This may take up to a minute
analyze.aiNotConfigured: AI is not configured. Ask the admin to set the OpenRouter API key.
admin.ai.title: AI / Deploy Agent
admin.ai.description: Configure the AI-powered Deploy Agent that analyzes repositories and generates Dockerfiles. Uses OpenRouter to access multiple AI models.
admin.ai.apiKeyHint: Create an API key at openrouter.ai/keys
admin.cleanup.manualDescription: Remove stopped containers, unused images, and build cache. Running containers and their images are preserved.
admin.cleanup.autoDescription: Automatically clean Docker resources when disk usage exceeds the threshold.
admin.cleanup.thresholdHint: Cleanup triggers when disk usage exceeds this percentage.
